name: Mostar Diving Platform
notes: A really cool spot with 3 different platform heights and some rocks nearby that can be jumped off. A great place to practice for the 22m bridge just upstream. Be careful of the cold water and the very strong tide. Also watch out for speedboats coming down the river.

name: Dancing Ledge
notes: An amazing crag just outside of Swanage with a variety of heights culminating in a big 15 metre jump marked by a white arrow at the top. This crag is also used by local coasteering companies so you know its a good cliff jumping spot. The approach is a 10-15 minute walk up/down a big hill. Keep in mind the depths vary with tide, especially the big 15m, be careful.

name: Old Bridge
notes: Popular Red Bull spot. Also pay the dive school to jump

name: Newquay Harbour
notes: A nice spot that has a lots of takeoffs and lots of heights protected by the harbour, but you can also choose to jump out to sea too. There is also some more takeoffs round the back of the harbour. Have an explore and have some fun. Be wary of boats and low tide.

name: La Grotta Bar
notes: A stunning cove with a diving board that isn't too high, perfect for a couple jumps while everyone at the bar watches and films you. There is also a rope attached to the cliff to the right of the board which can be used to climb up to a higher spot. Careful of the strong/big waves.

name: Blackbrook Reservoir
notes: Height varies rapidly with reservoir level. Depth check debris and large rocks underwater

name: Glyn Rhonwy/Mancer Quarry
notes: Quite hidden, access is through a tunnel I think

name: Playa Chica
notes: A concrete platform a couple metres high. In the morning it will be swarming with SCUBA divers. There is a concrete staircase for easy escape from the water.

name: Rhys & Peache's Cliff
notes: A collections of small/medium rocky cliffs on the East side of the tip of Portland. There are a bunch of jumpable spots that should all be depth checked first. There's lots of seaweed underwater so it looks slightly grim, but it's fine to jump into.

name: Smith and Morehouse Reservoir
notes: Not exact location, rope swing on east side of reservoir. Swing also possibly cut down.

name: Pine Meadow Lake
notes: Possible other spots around the lake

name: Joe Lake
notes: Not exact location, exploring of the west side of the island is needed

name: Primosten
notes: Popular and potentialy best spot to learn

name: Vermont Quarry
notes: One of the world's most iconic cliff jumping spots

name: Seebe Cliffs
notes: Very dangerous current, and not confirmed spot

name: Magdalen Bridge
notes: Depth check, lots of broken bones.

name: Las Pailas
notes: There's also a natural waterslide here

name: Ponte dei Salti
notes: Can also jump from the rock underneath the bridge or the roof just a little east of the bridge

name: Murau Bridge
notes: Or if you're feeling brave try the gappy jump over the railing from the round slab next the bridge in the grass

name: Carvalho Beach B
notes: Go up the steps on the side of the beach in the cliff, and then follow the path down to the little cave with a bench, then jump!

name: Barton Springs Free Area
notes: Needs some searching, but it's possible it's been taken down.

name: Pont-y-Pair Bridge
notes: It is illegal to jump off this, you could get a fine if you're caught.

name: River Wey Footbridge
notes: The platform is the brick wall next to the bridge.

name: Madonna sul Garda
notes: Can also jump from the handrail on the bridge.
